texto: This union has been divided in like a civil war - brother against brother - sister against sister. And I'm pulling it together. We've already seen evidence of that in New York, in Pennsylvania, in California. The first thing is we have to get on the same page. We have to be united in one cause.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a James P. Hoffa: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “This union has been divided in like a civil war - brother against brother - sister against sister…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, sister, brother pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de brother e together.
